products.name: LangChain
products.summary: The default OSS stack for chains, tools, and retrieval—LangGraph added stateful, graph-shaped agent workflows.

products.name: LlamaIndex
products.summary: Data-ingestion and indexing framework for document RAG—often the second name beside LangChain in app repos.

products.name: AutoGPT
products.summary: The 2023 viral autonomous loop—goals, tools, and memory in a repeating script—that made “AI agent” a household builder meme.

products.name: CAMEL
products.summary: Communicative Agents framework for role-playing and multi-agent simulation—an early open stack for agent societies.

products.name: CrewAI
products.summary: Python framework for crews of role-specialized agents working shared tasks—a popular multi-agent workflow brand.

products.name: OpenClaw
products.summary: Local-first personal AI assistant with a gateway for messaging channels, sandboxed tools, Live Canvas, and bundled skills—the viral open agent stack of 2026.

products.name: Hermes
products.summary: Nous Research’s self-improving agent runtime—persistent memory, autonomous skill creation, MCP-native tools, and multi-channel gateways on a VPS or serverless backend.

products.name: Qwen
products.summary: Alibaba’s open-weight model family—strong multilingual and coding variants with permissive licenses that made it a default self-host choice in Asia and beyond.

products.name: DeepSeek
products.summary: High-efficiency open models from DeepSeek—R1 reasoning and V3-class checkpoints that reset price/performance expectations for OSS deployments.

products.name: GLM
products.summary: Zhipu’s ChatGLM / GLM line—long-context Chinese-English models with open weights and a large domestic builder ecosystem.

products.name: Kimi
products.summary: Moonshot’s Kimi assistant and models—marketed on very long context and document-heavy workflows, with open components in the Moonshot stack.

products.name: MiniMax
products.summary: MiniMax’s open and API model family—speech, video, and text products from one of China’s most visible multimodal labs.

products.name: GPT
products.summary: OpenAI’s GPT / ChatGPT product line—the benchmark closed API and consumer app that still anchors “frontier model” mindshare.

products.name: Claude
products.summary: Anthropic’s Claude models and apps—safety-forward assistants and APIs widely used for coding, analysis, and agent backends.

products.name: Gemini
products.summary: Google’s Gemini family across Search, Workspace, and Vertex—closed multimodal APIs with selective open-weight releases on the side.

products.name: Grok
products.summary: xAI’s Grok—real-time, X-integrated assistants and APIs positioned as an alternative closed frontier stack.

products.name: Seed
products.summary: ByteDance’s Seed / Doubao model line—closed consumer and enterprise assistants built on one of China’s largest distribution surfaces.
